purpose: Reusable Kubernetes node naming convention for proxmox-k3s-generated clusters that must remain isolated from the historical sc-* cluster identity.

appliesTo: provider-backed clusters that are created, validated, benchmarked and then retained or destroyed according to the declared lifecycle policy
C4 provider-backed placement-variation profiles that reuse the same genai-pb naming convention while changing only application placement
C5 latency-injection infrastructure profiles that reuse the genai-pb naming convention while varying network conditions
C6 multi-tenant infrastructure profiles that reuse the genai-pb naming convention while varying application tenancy

namingRules.toolNameComposition: proxmox-k3s builds final node names as <cluster_name>-<configured_node_name> unless the configured name already starts with the same prefix.

methodologicalRationale: The historical fixed-cluster identity remains bound to sc-master-01, sc-app-01 and sc-app-02.
Provider-backed clusters are disposable or reusable operational artifacts and must not collide with historical node identities.
A single reusable provider-backed prefix keeps C1, C2 and C3 configurations simple while allowing one active provider-backed cluster at a time.
operationalRequirements: Set cluster_name to genai-pb in provider-backed proxmox-k3s configurations.
Use cp-01 for the first control-plane configured node name.
Use worker-01, worker-02 and subsequent worker-NN names for worker nodes.
Do not keep multiple provider-backed clusters with the same genai-pb prefix active at the same time unless VM names, IP ranges and lifecycle operations are explicitly isolated.
Do not use sc-* names for provider-backed create/delete workflows.

riskControls.historicalClusterCollisionRisk: The genai-pb-* prefix prevents create/delete operations from targeting the historical sc-* VM and Kubernetes node names.
riskControls.providerBackedReuseRisk: Before creating a new provider-backed cluster with the same prefix, ensure that the previous provider-backed cluster has been intentionally retained for reuse or deleted according to the lifecycle policy.
riskControls.nodeIdentityRisk: Do not infer hardware equivalence from node names; always use the declared infrastructureProfileId in run metadata and reports.
